content:
# Review

## Outcome

**Approved**

## Evidence

- All 98 milestone_record tests pass (0 failures, 0 ignored) — verified by running cargo test --lib contexts::milestone_record
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes cleanly (no warnings)
- cargo fmt --all --check passes (no formatting issues)
- Amendment fr-13-36f0aa72 FIXED: compare_task_run_recency (model.rs:379-381) now compares only started_at timestamps. latest_task_runs_per_bead (model.rs:386-406) uses `ordering != Ordering::Less` (line 394) so when started_at values are equal, later entries in the slice win. This means a running retry appended after a terminal entry with the same timestamp correctly outranks it, keeping the snapshot active.
- Amendment fr-13-36f0aa72 model regression: latest_task_runs_per_bead_prefers_later_same_timestamp_retry_state (model.rs:877-915) seeds [Failed@T, Running@T] and verifies Running wins. Also collapse_task_run_attempts_preserves_same_timestamp_legacy_retries (model.rs:1013-1070) seeds 4 same-timestamp legacy rows and verifies 2 distinct collapsed attempts.
- Amendment fr-13-36f0aa72 integration regression: same_timestamp_retry_keeps_snapshot_active (service.rs:1984-2059) runs start→fail→retry-at-same-T, asserts snapshot.status==Active, in_progress_beads==1, failed_beads==0, completed_beads==0 — confirming the running retry is correctly selected as the latest state.
- Amendment fr-13-a74a46a6 FIXED: find_matching_running_task_run (model.rs:416-443) now ignores the _started_at parameter entirely. For run_id=None, line 438-439 matches any sole running entry `[entry] => Some(entry.clone())` regardless of timestamp, enabling recovery when the retry uses a different started_at.
- Amendment fr-13-a74a46a6 model regression: find_matching_running_task_run_reuses_sole_runless_attempt_for_retry_repair (model.rs:944-971) seeds a Running entry at started_at=T, calls find_matching with started_at=T+30s and run_id=None, verifies it returns the existing entry.
- Amendment fr-13-a74a46a6 integration regression: start_retry_without_run_id_repairs_partial_start_write (service.rs:2900-2970) uses FailFirstJournalAppend mock to simulate journal failure on first start. First start fails, leaving a Running lineage row. Retry with different started_at (now+5s) reuses the existing running row (runs remain 1), snapshot is valid with active_bead present.
- model.rs: TaskRunEntry retains all required fields — milestone_id, bead_id, project_id, run_id, plan_hash, outcome, outcome_detail, started_at, finished_at — with serde(default, skip_serializing_if) for backward compatibility
- service.rs: reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage (lines 163-226) uses latest_task_runs_per_bead to rebuild counters from canonical lineage, transitions Active→Ready when no active beads remain, preserves Paused status
- service.rs: All mutation paths (update_status, persist_plan, record_bead_start, update_task_run) run under with_milestone_write_lock for serialized access
- All prior acceptance criteria remain satisfied: milestone state answers 'what happened to bead X?', controller can update linkage after each run outcome, backward compatibility maintained

## Findings and Gaps

- Both final-review amendments (fr-13-36f0aa72 and fr-13-a74a46a6) have been addressed with code changes and regression tests in completion round 14.
- The _started_at parameter in find_matching_running_task_run is now entirely unused (prefixed with underscore). While this is functionally correct for the recovery path, it slightly widens the matching surface — a sole runless running entry will now be reused by ANY retry regardless of timestamp gap. This is acceptable because: (a) the function only runs on already-collapsed canonical task runs, (b) only a single open entry can exist per bead/project, and (c) the milestone mutation lock prevents concurrent creation of ambiguous state.
- AppError::RunStartFailed is still used (service.rs ~line 555) for rejecting non-terminal outcomes in update_task_run, which is semantically a finalization rejection. A more specific error variant would be cleaner but this is cosmetic, not a correctness issue — carried forward from prior reviews.
- The outstanding risk — milestone mutations span separate lineage, snapshot, and journal files without a cross-file transaction — remains. All paths are serialized by the milestone mutation lock and replay-safe, making this an acceptable trade-off for file-based storage.
- Legacy attempts that still omit run_id remain inherently ambiguous for completion updates when multiple same-project attempts are open at once; this round improves single-open retry recovery but does not make multi-open legacy finalization deterministic.

## Follow-up and Amendments

None.
